reveals 2023 ticket sales |first=Ellie |last=Calnan |date=28 February 2023 |access-date=28 February 2023 |website=[[ScreenDaily]] |language=en |archive-date=1 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230301033706/https://www.screendaily.com/news/berlinale-sets-2024-dates-reveals-2023-ticket-sales/5179728.article |url-status=live }} ==Background== The film registration for the festival began in September 2022 with the closing date for submission fixed on 23 November 2022.{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/film-entry/submission/start.html |title=Welcome to the Film Entry Together |work=Berlinale |date=10 July 2022 |access-date=28 February 2023 |archive-date=28 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230228160910/https://www.berlinale.de/en/film-entry/submission/start.html |url-status=live }} On 13 October 2022, with the slogan of "Let's Get Together", signifying in-person interactions in all the programmes of the festival after two years of the programmes held online due to COVID-19. It was also announced that Berlinale Series Award would be inaugurated in 2023.{{cite web |url=https://www.screendaily.com/news/berlinale-confirms-in-person-european-film-market-for-2023/5175427.article |title=Berlinale confirms in-person European Film Market for 2023 |first=Orlando |last=Parfitt |date=13 October 2022 |access-date=28 February 2023 |website=[[ScreenDaily]] |language=en |archive-date=15 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230315195900/https://www.screendaily.com/news/berlinale-confirms-in-person-european-film-market-for-2023/5175427.article |url-status=live }}{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2023/news-press-releases/216898.html |title=Berlinale 2023: "Let's Get Together" |work=Berlinale |date=13 October 2022 |access-date=28 February 2023 |archive-date=28 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230228160922/https://www.berlinale.de/en/2023/news-press-releases/216898.html |url-status=live }} The festival poster was designed by Claudia Schramke, who had also created the previous year's Berlinale poster. The executive director of the Berlinale, {{ill|Mariette Rissenbeek|de|Mariette Rissenbeek}}, described the poster as the key visual for the 2023 Berlinale, saying that it directed the attention toward the audience—the indispensable core of the festival. Rissenbeek said, "We're very pleased that with this year's poster, we can honour those whose curiosity, enthusiasm, and applause make the Berlinale a vibrant, inspiring, and joyful event."{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2023/news-press-releases/223323.html |title=The 2023 Berlinale Key Design |work=Berlinale |date=20 December 2022 |access-date=1 March 2023 |archive-date=1 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230301061129/https://www.berlinale.de/en/2023/news-press-releases/223323.html |url-status=live }} ==Opening and closing ceremonies== [[File:MKr28562 Nicolas Philibert (Golden Bear, Berlinale 2023).jpg|thumb|right|[[Nicolas Philibert]], with the [[Golden Bear]] on the red carpet in front of the [[Berlinale Palast]]]] The opening ceremony of the festival was held on 16 February with jury members and international and German stars walking the red carpet. The jury president [[Kristen Stewart]], in her opening speech, pointed out about "oppressions against our physical selves". She said that, albeit she was a woman, she represented "the least marginal version of a woman". On that topic, [[Golshifteh Farahani]], Iranian-French actress and one of the jury members, noted that "some women are not as fortunate". [[Russo-Ukrainian War|Russia's war on Ukraine]] and [[Human rights in the Islamic Republic of Iran]] were topical discussions at the ceremony. After Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appeared via satellite and introduced by American actor and filmmaker [[Sean Penn]], Zelenskyy remarked, "A logical question comes up: On which side should culture and art be?" and added, "Can art be outside of politics? Should cinema be outside of politics? It's an eternal question but today it is extremely [pertinent]." Afterward, the opening film of the festival, Rebecca Miller's drama film ''She Came to Me'', was screened.{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2023/film/festivals/glamour-politics-berlin-film-festival-1235525618/|title=Berlin Film Festival Starts Strong With Zelenskyy, Kristen Stewart and Germaphobic Anne Hathaway|first1=Patrick|last1=Frater|first2=Manori|last2=Ravindran|first3=Ramin|last3=Setoodeh|date=16 February 2023|access-date=28 February 2023|work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|archive-date=16 February 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230216212307/https://variety.com/2023/film/festivals/glamour-politics-berlin-film-festival-1235525618/|url-status=live}} [[File:Sofia Otero with Silver Bear Berlinale 2023.jpg|thumb|right|Sofía Otero, with the [[Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance]] for ''[[20,000 Species of Bees]]'']] The closing ceremony or award night was held on 25 February, hosted by the German radio and television presenter Hadnet Tesfai. French documentary film ''[[On the Adamant]]'', about a Paris daycare centre for people with mental disorders, by Nicolas Philibert won the [[Golden Bear]]. Stewart called the film "masterfully crafted" and a "cinematic proof of the vital necessity of human expression". Philibert asked in his acceptance speech if the jury members were "crazy" and yet nevertheless thanked them, saying "that documentary can be considered to be cinema in its own right touches me deeply". The [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]] was won by ''[[Afire]]'' by [[Christian Petzold (director)|Christian Petzold]]. Sofía Otero, a nine-year-old girl was the winner of [[Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance]] for the role of eight-year-old Lucía in ''[[20,000 Species of Bees]]''. She became the youngest winner of the gender-neutral award in the Berlinale history. Stewart commented that Otero had defied "a system designed to diminish the intelligence of the performer", particularly that of child performers. After the ceremony, Otero said to the press that she wanted to dedicate her life to acting. The ceremony closed with a screening of the Golden-Bear winning film ''On the Adamant''.{{Cite web |url= https://www.nytimes.com/2023/02/25/movies/berlin-film-festival-winners.html |title= French
